profile.name: Qijia Liao
profile.description: I am a quantitative marketing scholar employing experimental design, causal inference, and machine learning to examine strategic marketing phenomena in digital environments. My research integrates marketing strategy, consumer behavior, and artificial intelligence.

research.intro: My research lies at the intersection of marketing strategy and consumer behavior, with a focus on digital marketing, brand activism, and artificial intelligence in marketing. I employ quantitative methods—experimental design, causal inference, and machine learning—to investigate strategic phenomena in digital environments.

streams.title: Brand Activism
streams.body: Investigating brand activism in non-Western institutional contexts and its impact on consumer and investor behavior. My work introduces the concept of System-Threat Activism (STA) and examines its effects on consumer responses through identity-threat mechanisms.

streams.title: AI, Digital Platforms & Marketing Analytics
streams.body: Examining artificial intelligence and algorithmic systems in digital marketing environments using large-scale data and deep learning. This stream explores influencer marketing effectiveness and algorithmic valuation anomalies in digital platforms.

publications.authors: Liao, Q., Yang, L., & Lim, E. J. (2023)
publications.title: A bibliometric and content analysis of marketing-finance interface research from 1986–2021

publications.authors: Liao, Q., Byrom, J., Guenther, M., & Song, F.
publications.title: When Brand Activism Threatens Consumers' Identity: An Investigation of System-Threat Activism

teaching.intro: I have accumulated 300 working hours as a Teaching Assistant across undergraduate and postgraduate courses at the University of Liverpool. Approximately 80% of my TA work involves seminar and online support for statistics, text analysis, and sentiment analysis using RStudio. I also handle marking of assignments, proposals, infographics, and presentations.
teaching.philosophy: I focus on hands-on support, helping students develop analytical skills through practical engagement with real-world marketing problems and industry datasets. My approach emphasises active learning and problem-solving, enabling students to generate actionable managerial insights from data.

courses.title: Marketing & Digital Analytics
courses.description: 100–150 students · Seminar support and online tutoring of RStudio: debugging, code interpretation, text analysis, and sentiment analysis.

courses.title: Market Research
courses.description: ~100 students · Supporting seminars on applied statistics; one-hour sessions on experimental methods. Marking market research proposals.

courses.title: Fundamentals of Marketing
courses.description: Marking assignments covering core marketing concepts and principles.

courses.title: Corporate Communications
courses.description: Marking assignments and presentations on corporate communication strategies.

interests: AI in Marketing / ML for Business

contact.job_market: Available for Assistant Professor or Postdoctoral positions in Marketing (August 2026 or earlier). Please feel free to reach out — I welcome inquiries about my research, teaching, and availability.
